programmes.show_description: Cristian Macelaru conducts the WDR Symphony Orchestra in Elgar's choral masterpiece, featuring Jamie Barton, John Findon, Derek Walton, the WDR Radio Choir and Berlin Radio Choir.
Mark Forrest presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Sarah Walker with three hours of classical music to reflect, restore and refresh, whatever the weather.
Michael Berkeley's guest is the poet and novelist Rachel Eliza Griffiths.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch explores one of Mozart's greatest hits, the Piano Sonata in A major. Featuring beloved music such as Beethoven's Für Elise and Turkish rhythms from Dave Brubeck.
...Classical Stories of Musical Theatre. Tenor Nicky Spence explores the deep and often-overlooked connections between classical music and the world of musical theatre.
Alyn Shipton presents jazz of all styles including music from Sonny Rollins, Cleo Laine & Dave O'Higgins.
Hannah French is joined in the studio by Edward Blakeman, who has just written a new biography of the early music specialist and broadcaster David Munrow, who died 50 years ago.
From Christ Church Moss Side, Manchester, with the Choral Scholars of St Martin-in-the-Fields and Diocese of Manchester Choral Scholars.
An exploration of the musical versatility of Richard Rodney Bennett - composer, pianist, jazz musician, cabaret singer, film score writer and avant-gardist.
Yasmin Mwanza and Sam Swann with Readings and Music go through the gates from the memorial to the metaphysical, taking us across fields and city walls.
2/12. Drift away with Erland Cooper and a selection of music that ebbs and flows, like tides lapping at the shoreline. Plus composer Anna Meredith shares her musical safe haven.
Hannah Peel presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Elizabeth Alker with a late-night journey through new music from today's most exciting genre-defying artists.
Violinist Leila Josefowicz performs with the Berlin Radio Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Roderick Cox, in Stravinsky's concerto. Penny Gore presents.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
The Arod Quartet plays Haydn & Tchaikovsky in a live recital from Wigmore Hall, plus Brahms and Liszt from the Ulster Orchestra, and pianist Elisabeth Leonskaja plays Shostakovich.
1/5. Donald Macleod enters the Parisian world of Frederic Chopin. Today, the young composer tries to get himself noticed on the city's exclusive salon circuit.
Petroc Trelawny presents live music in the studio from pianist Filippo Gorini and Gaelic singer Ruairidh Gray.
Wind down with half an hour of back to back classical music, including works by Rachmaninov, Bach, and Grant Still.
The BBC Philharmonic is joined by soprano Sarah Wegener for music by Wagner and Strauss. The orchestra gives the UK premiere of Edmund Finnis's The Landscape Wakes.

programmes.show_name: The Essay: Martin Hayes Turns the Tune
programmes.show_description: In conversation with Olivia O'Leary, Martin Hayes describes learning the fiddle as traditional musicians have for centuries and regional styles in different parts of Ireland.
Hannah Peel presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Soweto is back with another week of the best in jazz, and he welcomes leading saxophonist Mark Lockheart as his 4/4 guest.
